tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57979,"title":"[MySql Provisioning] Docs: Add examples and complete README for Azure.Provisioning.MySql","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` **Service directory:** `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #57773) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Introduced a new `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (1.0.0-beta.1) using the new provisioning generator. The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types: - `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` - `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` - `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration` - `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator` - `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2` - `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance` - `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability` - `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection` - `AdvancedThreatProtection` ## Gaps Found - **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library - **`Examples` section is empty** — no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc. - **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow - **No sample files exist** — `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`** Replace the empty sections with content as follows: 1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed — the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI. 2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule. 3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to: - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation Example README diff for the `## Examples` section: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a MySQL Flexible Server ``` Infrastructure infra = new(); MySqlFlexibleServer server = new("mysqlServer", MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30) { Location = AzureLocation.EastUS, Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku { Name = "Standard_D4ds_v4", Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose, }, }; infra.Add(server); ``` ### Add a Firewall Rule ``` MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new("allowAll") { StartIpAddress = "0.0.0.0", EndIpAddress = "255.255.255.255", Parent = server, }; infra.Add(firewallRule); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs` Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should: - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` Suggested snippets: - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` — demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape. 2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format: ```` ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. Currently missing/incomplete sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the Client | ❌ Empty | | Key concepts | ✅ Present (brief) | | Examples | ❌ Empty | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. For the **Authenticate the Client** section, adapt the pattern from other provisioning packages (e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): note that this library generates infrastructure-as-code (Bicep) and authentication is handled at deployment time via `azd` or ARM. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24175410555/agentic_workflow) · ● 432.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57979","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T06:18:01Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T06:18:01Z"}

The file should: - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` Suggested snippets: - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` — demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer` - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase` Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape. 2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format: ```` ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. For the **Authenticate the Client** section, adapt the pattern from other provisioning packages (e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): note that this library generates infrastructure-as-code (Bicep) and authentication is handled at deployment time via `azd` or ARM. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; verify the README diff looks correct 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 mysql` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24175410555/agentic_workflow) · ● 432.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57979","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-09T06:18:01Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T06:18:01Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents documentation gaps in the newly introduced `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` package (1.0.0-beta.1). The README has empty sections for authentication, examples, and next steps, and no sample test files exist for snippet extraction.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.MySql` 1.0.0-beta.1
**Affected API:** README.md sections and `tests/Samples/` directory
**Scenarios:**
- `Authenticate the Client` section in README is empty — no guidance on how authentication works with provisioning libraries
- `Examples` section is empty — no code snippets for creating a MySQL Flexible Server, firewall rule, database, etc.
- `Next steps` section is empty — no links to related docs, samples, or `azd` workflow
- No sample files exist under `tests/Samples/` — no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction

**Root ask:** Fill the empty README sections and create sample test files (NUnit) with `#region Snippet:` blocks under `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/` so `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject live code examples into the README.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This issue was triggered by PR #57773 (commit f36c2590), which introduced the new provisioning package. The issue is a documentation gap, not a code defect.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Check `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md` and `tests/Samples/` for the canonical provisioning sample structure to replicate
2. Check `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for README section formatting and tone
3. Create `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs` with `[Test]` methods and `#region Snippet:MySql...` / `#endregion` wrapping, then run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 mysql` to inject snippets into README placeholders
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — the package name (`Azure.Provisioning.MySql`) explicitly starts with `Azure.Provisioning`, matching the Provisioning category definition
- **Service:** `MySQL` — the package targets Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers; the `%MySQL` service label exists in CODEOWNERS at line 776
- **Confidence:** High — both labels are unambiguously determined by the package name; no other category or service is plausible
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %MySQL` (line 776) — scanned bottom-to-top; this was the first entry where all listed labels ("MySQL") are present in the issue's predicted labels
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ajlam @ambhatna @kummanish
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; `@mention`ed ServiceOwners via routing comment (no assignment since only ServiceOwners listed; no `needs-team-attention` since issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Lines 1212–1228 — `%CodeGen %Provisioning` and other PR-label entries — not ServiceLabel entries, skipped; line 891–892 — PR label for `/sdk/**/Azure.Provisioning*/`, not a ServiceLabel entry, skipped; line 776 `# ServiceLabel: %MySQL` — "MySQL" matches → STOP. The `# ServiceLabel: %Provisioning` at line 415 was not reached.
</details>
